questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

questions.question: Look at paragraph 14. What confused historians about how Hatshepsut wrote about herself?

responses.content:
The first way Hatshepsut's reign was different from the other two queens was she had a valid claim to the throne. But most importantly, Hatshepsut had the courage "to proclaim herself pharaoh." These are some reasons why her reign was different.

questions.question: Which of the following inferences is best supported by the excerpt?

responses.content: B. Hatshepsut, like many rulers, was a pharaoh first and her maternal duties came second.

questions.question: Which sentence from the excerpt most strongly supports the correct answer to Question 1?

responses.content: C. “There is no reliable record of exactly when or how it happened, but at some point, Hatshepsut took a bold and unprecedented step: She had herself crowned pharaoh with the large, heavy, red-and-white double crown of the two Egypts, north and south.”
